intent: Write a 3-source consumer report on 'sustainable household swaps' — products, community debate, and factual environmental framing. (1) One Stop Market shopping site — browse home-kitchen and health-household categories and collect at least 4 products marketed or applicable as reusable/eco alternatives (e.g. reusable containers, cloth products, refillable cleaners). Record name, price, rating, review count. (2) Reddit — browse /f/LifeProTips AND /f/science for posts on greenwashing, actual impact of household swaps, or which changes matter most. Collect at least 3 posts with title, score, comment count. (3) Wikipedia — open 'Sustainability' and 'Environmental impact of paper' (or similar relevant pages) and extract 3-4 facts. The report must: (a) list the storefront swaps with stats, (b) rank them by likely environmental impact using Wikipedia and community reasoning, (c) flag any storefront product that looks like greenwashing under community scrutiny, (d) close with a top-3 'actually worth it' list. Cite every claim.
author_notes: Hard 3-site. Sustainability + greenwashing check + impact ranking.
